title: myosotis sylvatica subsp. teresiana
intro: Myosotis sylvatica subsp. teresiana, also known as alpine forget-me-not, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds. It has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
